scenario: Forge Insurance writes a custom Camunda 8.8 outbound connector to invoke a private REST API for 7000 transactions per day. The connector receives configuration via the element template and reads variables from the job; the team must implement the contract the connector runtime expects on each invocation.
question: Which Java interface or annotation does a Camunda 8 outbound connector implementation provide?

options.text: Implement OutboundConnectorFunction with the execute(OutboundConnectorContext) method; the runtime calls execute on each activated job for that type
Implement JobHandler with the handle(JobClient, ActivatedJob) method; the connector runtime treats every outbound connector as a job worker class
Annotate a Spring bean with @ConnectorFunction(type = "my-connector"); the runtime scans for the annotation and invokes the bean as the connector
Extend BaseConnector and override invoke(Map<String,Object>); the runtime instantiates the class and calls invoke for each activated job

a.text: Correct. Outbound connectors in Camunda 8 implement OutboundConnectorFunction and define execute(OutboundConnectorContext) which the runtime calls per activated job.

b.text: Incorrect. JobHandler is the lower-level job worker interface; outbound connectors implement OutboundConnectorFunction instead.

c.text: Incorrect. The Connector SDK uses OutboundConnectorFunction discovery (e.g., service loader or annotations on the function class), not @ConnectorFunction on Spring beans.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no BaseConnector class with invoke(Map); the supported contract is OutboundConnectorFunction.execute.

explanation: Outbound connectors implement OutboundConnectorFunction; the runtime calls execute(OutboundConnectorContext) on each activated job for that connector type.
